commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: fix: copying text from AI chat no longer pastes block JSON
commits.body:
When text was selected (e.g. in the AI chat panel) while a block was
also selected on the canvas, Cmd+C would copy the block's JSON instead
of the highlighted text. The copy shortcut now checks for an active text
selection and yields to native copy when one exists.

Closes ETC-563

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: Revert "feat: condition-prop (ENABLE_CONDITON_PROP)"
commits.body:
This commit is a squash of a rather large feature branch. It's hard to review like this, and didn't follow process (one acceptance criteria -> one commit). Feels risky to keep.

We'll go through the changes on the feature branch on Monday morning, and push them as multiple commits, TBD-style.

This reverts commit a91d82e885f92c3bb3e64f0f08277dc181eb4f82.

ETC-587.title: Add condition Instance input
ETC-587.description:
The new design shall follow the design from figma

All the properties in the condition are rendered according to their type

The properties are hidden if the condition computes to false

The properties are visible if the condition computes to true

The condition itself will not render an accordion or conditional wrapper

ETC-551.title: ACSS stylesheets are no longer loaded into the builder if Yabe Webfont plugin is active
ETC-551.description:
## Summary

Third-party plugin stylesheets (ACSS, Yabe Font, etc.) stop rendering inside the builder canvas while still working on the frontend. Any plugin that appends stylesheets to the queue can trigger this — confirmed with Yabe Font but not plugin-specific.

**Root cause identified**: `normalize_assets_queue()` in `Hooks.php` calls `array_unique()` without `array_values()`, leaving non-sequential array keys. When `json_encode()` serializes this array, it silently converts it to an object, breaking the builder iframe's asset queue.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [X] Third-party plugin stylesheets (e.g. ACSS, Yabe Font) render correctly inside the builder canvas
- [X] Adding or removing plugins that register stylesheets does not break existing asset loading in the builder

ETC-585.title: Add Group Property Instance Input
ETC-585.description:
The design shall follow the new design form Figma

The user can see a collapsible group with the group name

All the properties in the group are rendered according to their type

The user can collapse the group to hide it

ETC-566.title: .toSlug() modifier strips initial umlauts instead of converting them
ETC-566.description:
## .toSlug() fails to properly handle German umlauts (ä, ö, ü) when they are positioned at the very beginning of a string. Instead of converting the character as stated in the documentation, it completely removes it.

### Steps to reproduce

Use the .toSlug() modifier on a string that starts with an umlaut.
For example, use the exact string from the official documentation: "Über Theme".

According to the documentation, the expected output for "Über Theme" is "uber-theme". But the first character is stripped entirely, resulting in the output "ber-theme".

While looking into this bug, I wanted to suggest a small enhancement regarding how umlauts are transliterated. Currently, the documentation states that umlauts drop their dots (e.g., "Ü" becomes "u"). The standard convention for transliterating German umlauts in URLs/slugs is actually to convert them to "ae", "oe", and "ue".

It would be fantastic if the .toSlug() modifier could be updated to reflect this standard. Ideally, "Über Theme" should become "ueber-theme" instead of "uber-theme".

---

### Environment

Plugin Version: 1.4.7

Theme Version: 0.0.7

WordPress Version: 6.9.4

Machine: PC

Browser: Chromium

---

### Magic Login

---

### Attachments

---

### Reporter

Name: Paul Gossen

Email: [paul@gosuccess.io](<mailto:paul@gosuccess.io>)

ETC-563.title: Copying text from AI chat panel pastes page JSON instead of selected text
ETC-563.description:
## Description

When selecting and copying text from the AI chat panel (e.g. a response from the AI) **while having a block selected**, pasting it elsewhere produces the JSON representation of the selected block instead of the copied text.

This is the same class of bug as [ETC-152](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-152/copying-text-from-canvas-pulls-copy-paste-json), which was fixed for the canvas. The clipboard interception that powers Etch's copy/paste system is too broad and captures copy events originating from the AI chat panel, overwriting the clipboard with Etch's internal JSON format.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open a page in the builder and **select a block** in the canvas
2. Open the AI chat panel and send a prompt
3. Select some text from the AI response
4. Copy it (Cmd+C / Ctrl+C)
5. Paste it anywhere (e.g. a text editor, browser URL bar)
6. Observe that the pasted content is JSON (page structure) instead of the text you selected

**NOTE: if you don't select a block, the bug will not be triggered.**

## Expected behavior

The copied text should be the actual text selected in the AI chat panel.

## Actual behavior

The clipboard contains the Etch page JSON instead of the selected text.

## Root cause analysis

The `copy` shortcut in `src/frontend/Builder/src/composables/stores/shortcuts/builder/copyPaste/index.ts` intercepts Cmd+C when all of the following are true:

1. `!focusWatcher.isInsideEditableText()` — the active element is not an input/textarea/contentEditable
2. `context.active === 'builder'`
3. `!(builder.selectedBlock instanceof EtchFocusBlock)`

When the user selects text from the AI response (which is rendered HTML, not an editable element), condition 1 passes. Combined with a selected block and builder context, the shortcut fires `copyBlock()`, which writes page JSON to the clipboard and calls `preventDefault()`, suppressing the browser's native copy.

**The bug only manifests when a block is selected.** If no block is selected, `onKeyTrigger` returns `false`, `preventDefault()` is not called, and native copy works normally. This is why the bug initially appeared to be fixed in 1.4.7 — testing without a selected block masked the issue.

A proper fix was attempted on the `experimental-ai-integration` branch (commit `05df0154`) which registered the AI chat as a focus container in `FocusWatcher`, but that branch was never merged to main.

## Suggested fix

**If the user has text selected in the main window, let native copy win.**

Check selection origin in the copy shortcut's condition — e.g., if `window.getSelection()` is anchored within the AI chat container, skip the shortcut:

```
const hasTextSelection = () => {
    const selection = window.getSelection();
    return selection && selection.toString().length > 0;
};
```
